discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -241,7 +243,7 @@ def select_llm_provider() -> tuple[str, str | None]:
         ("GLM", "glm", "https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4/"),
         ("OpenRouter", "openrouter", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"),
         ("Azure OpenAI", "azure", None),
-        ("Ollama", "ollama", "http://localhost:11434/v1"),
+        ("Ollama", "ollama", os.environ.get("OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434/v1")),
discussion_comments.comment_body:
Thanks for flagging this. I went with `os.environ.get(K, default)` deliberately and would gently push back:

If a user sets `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=` in their `.env` (empty value — typically because they forgot to fill it in), the current form passes an empty string to `ChatOpenAI`, which surfaces a clear configuration error. The `os.getenv(K) or default` form would silently fall back to `http://localhost:11434/v1`, which is the exact symptom this PR is trying to avoid: users wondering why their config "isn't doing anything" while requests still hit localhost.

Stylistically, this also matches the rest of the `PROVIDERS` list and the `_PROVIDER_CONFIG` dict in `openai_client.py`, which both use plain string literals for URLs.

Happy to switch if maintainers prefer the silent-fallback semantics, but I'd rather have an explicit failure than a quiet localhost.

commented_code:
+import unittest
+
+from cli.utils import CUSTOM_OPENROUTER_MODEL, resolve_model_choice
+
+
+class OpenRouterModelSelectionTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_builtin_model_is_returned_unchanged(self):
+        self.assertEqual(
+            resolve_model_choice("openrouter", "z-ai/glm-4.5-air:free", "Quick-Thinking"),
+            "z-ai/glm-4.5-air:free",
+        )
+
+    def test_custom_model_prompt_value_is_trimmed(self):
+        chosen = resolve_model_choice(
+            "openrouter",
+            CUSTOM_OPENROUTER_MODEL,
+            "Deep-Thinking",
+            prompt_fn=lambda _: " minimax/minimax-m2.1 ",
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(chosen, "minimax/minimax-m2.1")
+

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+import unittest
+
+from cli.utils import CUSTOM_OPENROUTER_MODEL, resolve_model_choice
+
+
+class OpenRouterModelSelectionTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_builtin_model_is_returned_unchanged(self):
+        self.assertEqual(
+            resolve_model_choice("openrouter", "z-ai/glm-4.5-air:free", "Quick-Thinking"),
+            "z-ai/glm-4.5-air:free",
+        )
+
+    def test_custom_model_prompt_value_is_trimmed(self):
+        chosen = resolve_model_choice(
+            "openrouter",
+            CUSTOM_OPENROUTER_MODEL,
+            "Deep-Thinking",
+            prompt_fn=lambda _: " minimax/minimax-m2.1 ",
+        )
+        self.assertEqual(chosen, "minimax/minimax-m2.1")
+
discussion_comments.comment_body:
Thanks! I’ve added failure-path coverage for the `None` / empty custom model cases in the OpenRouter model-selection tests.

For the `exit(1)` suggestion: this file already uses the same pattern in several existing helpers, so I kept this PR scoped to the OpenRouter selection change and test coverage. If maintainers prefer, I’m happy to follow up with a separate cleanup to standardize these exit paths with `typer.Exit`.
